skill.name: test-driven-development
skill.description: Guide coding agents through strict test-first development using the red, green, and refactor workflow.
skill.summary: Apply test-driven development before writing production code.
skill.author: DMJGilbert

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives caused by ordinary prose about desired behavior, over-engineering, and test status, with no executable code or operational instructions for reconnaissance. No prompt injection attempt, data exfiltration path, command execution, or hidden business logic issue was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 3 contains the skill description for a test-driven development workflow. The text does not reference cryptographic functions, libraries, keys, hashes, or algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain metadata describing the red, green, and refactor cycle. There is no cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm usage to evaluate.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 20 says to write one minimal failing test demonstrating desired behavior. This is testing guidance, not cryptographic code or a weak algorithm reference.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious match appears to come from normal English inside documentation. No crypto API, cipher name, or data protection behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 21 instructs users to use clear test names that describe behavior. It contains no cryptographic code, cryptographic configuration, or security-sensitive algorithm choice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is ordinary testing guidance and has no executable content. The alert is a semantic mismatch rather than a real weak-crypto signal.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 38 advises avoiding over-engineering during implementation. It does not instruct the agent to inspect the host, enumerate files, collect system data, or run reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is software design discipline, not environment discovery. No system command or target for reconnaissance appears on the line.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md line 44 says all tests should pass. It does not mention networks, scanning tools, remote hosts, URLs, ports, or connectivity checks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a normal test verification step. There is no evidence of network access or reconnaissance intent.

content.value_statement: Teams often write production code before tests, which makes regressions harder to catch. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a strict test-first workflow.
actual_capabilities: Requires a failing test before production code is written.
Guides the red, green, and refactor cycle for new work.
Checks that a new test fails for the expected reason.
Keeps implementation scope limited to the tested behavior.
Prompts a restart when code was written before a valid test.
Stops repeated test-fix attempts and asks for architectural review.
limitations: Does not provide framework-specific test templates.
Does not run tests by itself without an agent or tool command.
May slow throwaway prototypes where tests are not useful.
Requires user judgment for generated code and configuration exceptions.

use_cases.title: Build a New Feature Safely
use_cases.description: Start with one failing test, implement only enough code to pass, then refactor while keeping tests green.

use_cases.title: Fix a Bug With Regression Coverage
use_cases.description: Reproduce the bug as a failing test before changing production code.

use_cases.title: Guide Agentic Coding Sessions
use_cases.description: Keep AI coding assistants focused on test-first changes and narrow implementation scope.

prompt_templates.title: Start TDD for a Simple Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a small feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use test-driven development for this change. First write one failing test that proves the requested behavior is missing. Show why it should fail before editing production code.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Bug Test First
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to fix the bug. Start by writing a regression test that fails for the current behavior. Only change production code after the failure is confirmed.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor With Green Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve existing code without behavior changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the red, green, and refactor cycle. Confirm the current tests are green, add any missing behavior test first, then refactor while keeping all tests passing.

prompt_templates.title: Recover From Broken TDD Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow when code was written too early.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the current work against strict TDD. If production code was written before a failing test, identify the affected changes, reset the approach, and restart from the failing test.

output_examples.input: Add validation for an invalid email address.
output_examples.output: The agent first proposes a failing validation test, explains the expected failure, implements the smallest validation change, and then verifies all tests pass.

output_examples.input: Fix a date parsing bug found in production.
output_examples.output: The agent creates a regression test for the reported date case, confirms it fails for the current parser, applies the fix, and keeps the change narrow.

output_examples.input: Refactor a helper that already has tests.
output_examples.output: The agent confirms the existing tests are green, performs the refactor in small steps, reruns tests, and avoids changing behavior.

best_practices: Write one focused failing test before each behavior change.
Confirm the test fails for the expected reason before implementation.
Refactor only after all related tests pass.
anti_patterns: Writing production code before a failing test exists.
Adding broad tests that cover many behaviors at once.
Continuing repeated fixes after several test failures without reassessing design.

faq.question: Does this skill write tests for a specific framework?
faq.answer: No. It gives process rules that an agent can apply with the test framework already used by the project.

faq.question: Can I use it for bug fixes?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill asks for a failing regression test before changing production code.

faq.question: Does it allow prototypes without tests?
faq.answer: Yes, but only for throwaway prototypes, generated code, or configuration work with permission.

faq.question: What happens if the test passes immediately?
faq.answer: The skill says the test proves nothing and should be replaced with a valid failing test.

faq.question: Does it execute commands or access the network?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only and contains no scripts, commands, URLs, or network instructions.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
